summary: Linked the item translation unit src/melee/it/items/itseakchain.c by switching it from NonMatching to Matching, with small C declaration/local-name adjustments and symbols.txt local-label updates for nearby .sdata/.sdata2 entries. The bot report records 3 new matches for itseakchain: .sdata2, it_802BAF2C, and it_802BCB88.

key_files.role: Item source TU changed to support matching; added order_sdata2, adjusted declaration scope/order in it_802BAF2C, and renamed a local from prev to cur in it_802BCB88.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report lists main/melee/it/items/itseakchain matches

key_files.role: Build configuration changed itseakchain.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...).
evidence_refs: raw_diff: configure.py hunk around MatchingFor item object list

key_files.role: Symbol metadata changed several itseakchain-adjacent .sdata and .sdata2 globals to local labels.

what_changed.text: configure.py now treats melee/it/items/itseakchain.c as Matching instead of NonMatching.
Added a static order_sdata2 function containing void-cast float literals 0.0f, 0.1f, and 1.0f.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: src/melee/it/items/itseakchain.c added static void order_sdata2(void)

what_changed.text: In it_802BAF2C, moved link_gobj and link declarations out of the loop/block and adjusted declaration order around attrs, i, and zero.
In it_802BCB88, removed a separate prev local and reused/renamed the local ItemLink pointer as cur for the it_802BCB88_prev result and pos1 source.
symbols.txt changed two .sdata string entries and six .sdata2 float/double entries from global it_ address names to local @ labels.
The automated report recorded +1516 matched-code bytes, +8812 linked-code bytes, and 3 new matches: .sdata2, it_802BAF2C, and it_802BCB88 for main/melee/it/items/itseakchain.

smart_moves.text: The PR paired the Matching configuration flip with a bot report showing the specific new matches in the same unit, rather than only changing configure.py.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists 3 new matches

smart_moves.text: The source changes were narrow: declaration placement/name adjustments in two nearly matched functions plus a small data-order helper, with no broad rewrite shown in the diff.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For a nearly complete item TU, small declaration-scope and declaration-order changes can be enough to close remaining function mismatches when verified by a narrow report.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report shows it_802BAF2C from 99.48% to 100.00%

decomp_lessons.lesson: When local pointer role is clearer after a helper call, using a role-consistent local name can accompany a match cleanup, but the PR evidence only proves this specific it_802BCB88 cleanup.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: prev local removed, cur assigned from it_802BCB88_prev
text_corpus: bot report shows it_802BCB88 from 99.97% to 100.00%

naming_conventions.convention: Use local role names such as cur for the current ItemLink pointer when that role is supported by nearby traversal code.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: it_802BCB88 uses ItemLink* cur and removes ItemLink* prev

naming_conventions.convention: This PR converted some address-style data symbols to local @ labels where the symbols metadata treated the entries as local literals/constants.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: it_804D5508/it_804D5510 and it_804DD0D8-0x804DD0F0 changed to @ labels

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Promote a TU from NonMatching to Matching only after the narrow report shows the remaining unit/function/data matches.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists 3 new matches for main/melee/it/items/itseakchain
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: The PR slice includes bot report output but no human explanation of local build commands or objdiff invocation.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Declaration lifetime/scope steering: hoist link_gobj and link declarations out of a for-loop/block to adjust codegen for it_802BAF2C.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report shows it_802BAF2C reached 100.00%
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Global standards require narrow verifier evidence for declaration-order/lifetime tactics; this PR has bot report evidence but no detailed objdiff notes.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Data-order steering: add an otherwise unused order_sdata2 function with float literals to affect .sdata2 ordering.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: This resembles a data-order helper and should not be generalized without curator review because standards warn against fake storage or data-order churn unless justified.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Fix symbol metadata from global address-style names to local labels for literals/constants when section ownership requires local labels for matching.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: matched data +32 bytes and linked data +152 bytes
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: The PR evidence supports the specific symbols changes but does not explain the full ownership analysis.

follow_up_queries: data-order helper void cast float literals

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, state, author, changed files, counts
text_corpus.jsonl: empty PR body and decomp-dev bot report
notes: No human review comments were present in the loaded slice.
The PR body is empty, so author intent is inferred only from the title, diff, and bot report.
The bot report provides matching outcomes, but the slice does not include explicit local build, objdiff command, or adjacent-regression notes.
The order_sdata2 helper and symbols metadata changes are recorded as PR facts, not promoted as accepted global style.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Declaration-scope adjustment closed near-match functions in itseakchain
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2674, hoisting link_gobj/link declarations and adjusting local pointer use in src/melee/it/items/itseakchain.c accompanied reported 100% matches for it_802BAF2C and it_802BCB88. Treat as a narrow historical tactic with bot-report evidence, not as a default style rule.

source_update_candidates.title: itseakchain.c was linked as Matching in PR 2674
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2674 changed configure.py so melee/it/items/itseakchain.c is Object(Matching, ...) instead of Object(NonMatching, ...). The bot report lists new matches for .sdata2, it_802BAF2C, and it_802BCB88 in main/melee/it/items/itseakchain.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a source/path status fact supported by the PR diff and report; curator should decide whether path facts track historical matching promotions.

source_update_candidates.title: symbols.txt local-label changes for itseakchain-related .sdata/.sdata2 entries
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2674 changed config/GALE01/symbols.txt entries at 0x804D5508, 0x804D5510, and 0x804DD0D8 through 0x804DD0F0 from global it_ address symbols to local @ labels.
source_update_candidates.reason: Specific metadata changes are well-evidenced, but ownership rationale is not explained in the PR body.

source_update_candidates.title: Review data-order helper pattern before accepting or discouraging it
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2674 added static order_sdata2 with void-cast float literals and the bot report showed .sdata2 reached 100.00%. This may be a historical data-order tactic, but current standards caution against fake data/order churn. Curator review should decide whether to document it as an exception pattern, an anti-pattern, or only a path-specific historical note.
source_update_candidates.reason: The evidence supports that the tactic appeared in this PR, but not that it should become accepted global style.

rejection_notes: Do not infer broad approval for unused ordering helper functions from this PR alone.
Do not infer that converting string or float symbols from global address names to local @ labels is always correct; this PR only supports the touched addresses.
Do not claim human reviewer intent or review approval rationale; no review comments were loaded.
